title: Add top navigation bar with four cemetery sections

requirements.summary: Replace navigation tiles with a sticky top navigation bar containing four labeled sections (Mapa grobów, Wyszukiwanie, Modlitwa za zmarłych, Nasz cmentarz) for smooth section switching
acceptanceCriteria: A horizontal sticky navigation bar is visible at the top of the public page with exactly four items: Mapa grobów, Wyszukiwanie, Modlitwa za zmarłych, Nasz cmentarz.
Clicking any navigation item immediately activates and displays the corresponding section.
The active navigation item is visually distinguished (e.g., underline, highlight, or accent color) from inactive items.
Navigation transitions are smooth and do not cause full page reloads.
The bar is responsive and looks aesthetically appropriate on both desktop and mobile screen sizes.
The bar remains accessible (sticky) while scrolling through the page content.
Labels displayed match the Polish text: Mapa grobów, Wyszukiwanie, Modlitwa za zmarłych, Nasz cmentarz.

file_operations.description: Create a new sticky navigation bar component that renders four horizontally aligned navigation items with Polish labels (Mapa grobów, Wyszukiwanie, Modlitwa za zmarłych, Nasz cmentarz). The component accepts an activeSection prop and an onSectionChange callback. Style the bar to be fixed or sticky at the top of the content area (below the Header), with active item styling (e.g., border-bottom or background highlight) to indicate the current section. Ensure responsive design for both desktop and mobile using Tailwind CSS classes. The navigation items should be keyboard accessible and use semantic button elements.

Replace the PublicSectionsTiles component with the new PublicNavigationBar component. Add state management for the active section using useState. Wire the PublicNavigationBar's onSectionChange callback to update the active section state. Update the conditional rendering logic to show/hide the four content sections (grave map, search, prayer, cemetery info) based on the active section state. Ensure the navigation bar is placed immediately below the HeroSection and above all content sections. Remove the import and usage of PublicSectionsTiles entirely from this file.

requirements.summary: Remove the PublicSectionsTiles grid component from the public page while keeping all four content sections functional via the new navigation bar
acceptanceCriteria: The large navigation tile grid (PublicSectionsTiles) is no longer rendered on the public page.
All four content sections (grave map, search, prayer, cemetery info) remain accessible via the new navigation bar.
No visual duplication of navigation controls exists on the public page.

file_operations.description: Delete the PublicSectionsTiles component file as it is no longer needed. The navigation role is now fulfilled by the new PublicNavigationBar component created in REQ-1.
